title: Vulkan compute runtime dispatch via ash
goal: Realize CLAUDE.md anti-pattern #9 (no mocking GPU execution) by actually running emitted SPIR-V on a Vulkan compute queue via ash. Lavapipe (mesa software Vulkan) as CI fallback. Proves end-to-end: .axc source → HIR → SPIR-V → Vulkan shader module → compute pipeline → dispatch → readback → CPU verification. Unlocks all subsequent milestones' correctness claims.
scope_in: axc-runtime crate: real content (was placeholder M0 stub). Vulkan instance+device+queue+command pool setup via ash 0.38.
Physical device selection: prefer a device matching $AXC_PHYSICAL_DEVICE (env, device index), else first discrete GPU, else software Lavapipe (VK_DRIVER_FILES / VK_ICD_FILENAMES).
Device features: enable VK_KHR_subgroup (required for M1.4 output) + VK_KHR_storage_buffer_storage_class. Require device support; fall back to skipping tests if unsupported.

Public API: `pub fn dispatch(spirv: &[u32], bindings: &BindingPlan, workgroups: (u32,u32,u32), inputs: &[&[u8]], output_sizes: &[usize], push_constants: &[u8]) -> Result<Vec<Vec<u8>>, DispatchError>` -- single-shot dispatch, reads back output buffers as bytes.
Per binding: create host-visible SSBO (buffer + memory), memcpy input bytes, bind to descriptor set 0 at the correct binding index.
Push constants: map the scalar params to a flat byte blob per M1.2 layout; supplied by caller.
Descriptor set layout + descriptor pool + descriptor set allocation automatic from BindingPlan.
Compute pipeline creation: shader module (from spirv words), pipeline layout (descriptor set + push constant range), pipeline.
Command buffer recording: bind pipeline, bind descriptor set, push constants, vkCmdDispatch with workgroups, pipeline barrier (HOST_READ after SHADER_WRITE).
Submit to compute queue + wait on fence (synchronous; no async for M1.5).
Readback: map memory, copy bytes to Vec<u8>, return.
Correctness-oracle integration tests for saxpy and vector_add: compile .axc → dispatch → compare against CPU f32 reference with @equiv_fp_tol(1e-6)
CI: install mesa-vulkan-drivers + vulkan-tools on Ubuntu, export VK_DRIVER_FILES=/usr/share/vulkan/icd.d/lvp_icd.x86_64.json to force Lavapipe, run the integration tests — on actual GPU execution.
scope_out: Staging buffers / device-local memory heuristics (host-visible for M1.5 simplicity; M2 adds device-local + staging)
Windows / macOS (MoltenVK) CI — Linux Lavapipe only for M1.5
`axc run` CLI subcommand (keep API-only for M1.5; CLI is trivial wrapper added in M2)
acceptance_criteria: cargo test --workspace passes on CI with Lavapipe (expected floor ~420 after M1.5 additions; integration tests are ignored by default, gated on env var AXC_ENABLE_GPU_TESTS=1 + Lavapipe availability)
Integration test `dispatch_saxpy_matches_cpu_reference` passes when Lavapipe is available; skips gracefully otherwise with a printed note
DispatchError variants cover: VulkanInitFailed, NoSupportedDevice, ShaderCompilationFailed, BufferAllocationFailed, DescriptorSetFailed, CommandSubmitFailed, FenceTimeout, ReadbackFailed
Memory cap: total GPU-allocated buffers per test must not exceed 100 MB (tiny for M1.5)
Regression: all M0..M1.4 tests still pass (405 baseline)
open_questions_for_architect: ash 0.38 version pin: already in workspace.dependencies — confirm it's usable for Vulkan 1.1.
Lavapipe install method on Ubuntu CI: `apt install mesa-vulkan-drivers` should suffice. Confirm the ICD path.

BindingPlan serialization: currently axc-hir owns BindingPlan. Does axc-runtime depend on axc-hir directly, or do we define a minimal runtime-side BindingPlan struct to decouple? Recommend direct dep for M1.5 (simplest); refactor if needed in M2.

Workgroup count: should `axc compile` emit a metadata sidecar with @workgroup dims + binding plan? Or does the runtime re-parse the source? Recommend metadata sidecar (small JSON alongside .spv) so runtime doesn't re-parse.
Synchronous vs async dispatch API: synchronous for M1.5 (simple). M2 adds handle-based async.
